title: BP, Corteva Form a JV, Etlas to Make Feedstocks for Sustainable Aviation Fuel and Renewable Diesel
content: Corteva and bp have launched Etlas, a 50:50 joint venture designed to produce and deliver biofuel feedstocks for Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) and Renewable Diesel (RD). Leveraging Cortevaâ€™s seed technology and bpâ€™s refining expertise, Etlas aims to produce one million metric tonnes of feedstock annually by the mid-2030s, capable of generating over 800,000 tonnes of biofuel. The venture will utilize intermediate cropsâ€”such as canola, mustard, and sunflowerâ€”grown on existing farmland between main food seasons, providing farmers with new revenue streams without competing for land. Initial supply is scheduled to begin in 2027. Ignacio Conti has been appointed CEO, with Gaurav Sonar serving as Chair of the Board. The partnership targets the surging global demand for biofuels, with SAF demand projected to reach 10 million tonnes by 2030.
author: Analysts Account
